topic: Rate of fuel combustion vs altitude (presentation errors)
image_prompt: SVG: bar chart with deliberate presentation errors (axes swapped, bars instead of scatter). y-axis Altitude / m (0 to 5000, ticks every 500, minor every 100), x-axis categorical Rate of fuel combustion / g min⁻¹ with labels 0.153, 0.126, 0.103, 0.0850, 0.0774. Five teal bars at heights 850, 2400, 3100, 3900, 4600 m.

SVG: multi-series smooth line graph, x-axis Year (1900 to 2020, ticks every 10, minor every 5), y-axis Billion cubic metres per year (0 to 3500, ticks every 500, minor every 100). Six regional series, smooth curves, colours teal/orange/magenta/green/violet/cyan (no red): Asia (highest, largest increase 1900-2020, ~3050 at 2020), North America (reaches ~1000 at 1960, dramatic rise 1950-1980, ~1600 at 2020), Europe (~800), Africa (~610), South America (~420), Australia and Oceania (lowest, ~185).
